_comment: PIPE9 golden parity fixture for the transcript text-correction overlay. Both the Swift TranscriptCorrectionStoreTests and the Python test_transcript_corrections.py write each case's `corrections` payload to a `<stem>.transcript_corrections.json`, read it back through their store, apply it to the `segments`, and assert the resulting per-segment text equals `expected`. A deliberate change to either resolver breaks the other tree's test, so the Swift-to-Python contract cannot silently diverge. `index` is the position in the <stem>.json segments array (TranscriptSegment.index / Python enumerate). Hand-authored; keep both sides in sync.
